publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-26 18:01:56 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Professional papers of Walter LaFeber, a Cornell University historian specializing in American foreign policy.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Martin Heggestad, March 2003; Serge Agbre, September 2008

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Walter LaFeber graduated from Hanover College in 1955, received a master's degree from Stanford University in 1956 and a Ph.D. from the University of Wisconsin in 1959. He came to Cornell in 1963 and is currently the Marie Underhill Noll Professor of American History. He specializes in American foreign policy. His major works include The New Empire: An Interpretation of American Expansion, America; Russia and the Cold War; and The Panama Canal: The Crisis in Historical Perspective; as well as editing John Q. Adams and the American Continental Empire.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Professional papers relating to his research, teaching, and writing about American foreign policy.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Walter LaFeber Papers, #14-17-2624. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted until five years after the death of the donor and his spouse.

did.unittitle: Westminster Foundation; John Quincey Adams Manuscript; miscellaneous correspondence; Correspondence January-June 1976; old recommendations. [Poor condition.]
Correspondence. University stuff; Crowell book; CU Presidential Commission
Cornell Aeronautical Lab; Dartmouth Conference; Correspondence January-September 1969.
Correspondence. State Department Advisory Committee on Foreign Relation; Chelsea House Publisher) Correspondence. Pacific Historic Review.
Constitution correspondence, Correspondence from 1968, 1980, 1981, and some from 1978-1980
FDR Foundation; Reuben Askew for President file; file on BPA course 1981-1983; 1984 correspondence
OAH-Hanington Paper; 1985-1986 correspondence; Free Speech commission 1986-1987; Seward book
Book Contracts and permissions; July-December 1984; January-June 1985
July-December 1987; January-June 1988. Matthew McHugh Congress 1975-July-December 1986; a cassettes.
Correspondence 1975; July-December 1978; letters 1971; Pulitzer Prize July 1975
Miscellaneous (1982-February 1992) filed with folders for March-December 1992.
Miscellaneous (April 1970- December 1991) filed with folders for January-October 1992
"Harry Truman" PBS-TV-"The American Experience" program (4 hours)
Part three of Truman; Series themes
TR-draft script for parts 1, 2 and 3; correspondence
The American Experience - Theodore Roosevelt, Mar. 3, 1995: Program Script
Eric Delman phone conversations; his memos on Bush-Pentagon Russian Policy
Eric Delman's The End of the Cold War: The Triumph of Idealpolitik
Copy of letter on proposal of the committee on Diplomatic Documentation
Correspondence and reviews relating to "Inevitable Revolutionary: The United States in Central America"
Correspondence, newspaper articles and reviews relating to "Inevitable Revolutionary: The United States in Central America"
Presidency of Society of Historians of American Foreign Relations
